expectations.text: the agent should read skills/crafting-skills/SKILL.md before reviewing; the skill encodes the description-quality rubric
the review should present findings as a before/after diff (current description + suggested rewrite), not just 'consider improving X'
expectations.evidence: structure assertion without compare_args falls back to LLM judge

expectations.text: the reviewed skill's frontmatter should pass marketplace-validator (name format, description length ≤1024, license present, no stale platform refs)
expectations.evidence: structure assertion without compare_args falls back to LLM judge

expectations.text: the agent should apply the routing-quality checklist (Load when / Use when / Do NOT use for / concrete trigger phrases / negative scope) to the reviewed skill's description
expectations.evidence: The agent only asked 'Which skill would you like me to review?' and never applied any routing-quality checklist or analyzed any skill's description.

expectations.text: the suggested description rewrite should be measurably better for routing — i.e., include trigger phrases the user would actually type, and explicit negative scope to prevent over-triggering
expectations.evidence: No description rewrite was provided; the agent did not produce any suggested description, trigger phrases, or negative scope statements.
